channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
item.description: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now features a variety of third-party integrations, enhancing the capabilities of AI agents for real-world applications. This development allows users to connect their agents to popular tools like GitHub and Notion.

item.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
item.description: Google I/O 2026 is set for May 19-20, featuring a save the date puzzle that showcases AI's potential. This annual event builds excitement and highlights innovations in AI technology.

item.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
item.description: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas will focus on networking and hands-on problem-solving, emphasizing the shift to agentic AI. The event will feature technical tracks on various topics, enhancing developer skills.

item.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
item.description: Google has launched LiteRT, a new framework that significantly improves GPU and NPU acceleration for deploying GenAI. This update also enhances support for PyTorch and JAX, marking a shift in TensorFlow's capabilities.

item.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
item.description: Google Cloud's Wednesday Build Hour is an interactive session aimed at helping developers enhance their cloud skills through hands-on experience. The program focuses on advanced topics like AI agents and productivity tools.

item.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
item.description: The Gemini Code Assist team has rolled out updates to streamline coding workflows, introducing features like Agent Mode and Inline Diff Views. These tools aim to transform AI into a more personalized coding assistant.

item.title: Introducing Finish Changes and Outlines, now available in Gemini Code Assist extensions on IntelliJ and VS Code
item.description: Google's Gemini Code Assist now includes features like Finish Changes and Outlines in IntelliJ and VS Code, designed to reduce developer friction. These tools enhance code completion and comprehension.

item.title: Enforcing new limits and retiring Opus 4.6 Fast from Copilot Pro+
item.description: GitHub Copilot is implementing new usage limits and retiring the Opus 4.6 Fast model to manage high concurrency issues. This decision reflects the growing demand for AI coding assistance.

item.title: Snowflake manager explains the 'Spider-Man' theory of AI agent data access
item.description: Snowflake's director highlights the importance of clean and accessible data for AI agents, suggesting that data quality is a major bottleneck in AI development. This insight emphasizes the need for effective data governance.

item.title: Here's how to watch the Artemis II splashdown
item.description: NASA's Artemis II mission successfully returned astronauts from a historic journey beyond Earth, marking a significant achievement in space exploration amidst global challenges. This mission showcases advancements in space travel technology.

item.title: Copilot usage metrics now aggregate Copilot cloud agent active user counts
item.description: GitHub has updated its metrics to aggregate active user counts for its renamed Copilot cloud agent, enhancing data tracking for user engagement. This change reflects GitHub's ongoing efforts to improve its services.

item.title: Red Hat RHELocates its Chinese engineering team to India
item.description: Red Hat has laid off its entire engineering team in China, relocating most of them to India, indicating a shift in its geopolitical strategy. This move raises questions about the future of Red Hat's operations in China.

item.title: Anthropic temporarily banned OpenClaw’s creator from accessing Claude
item.description: Anthropic has temporarily banned the creator of OpenClaw from accessing its Claude AI after a recent pricing change. This action highlights the challenges of managing third-party integrations.

item.title: Pausing new GitHub Copilot Pro trials
item.description: GitHub is pausing new trials for Copilot Pro due to increased abuse of the free trial system, aiming to protect the platform's integrity for legitimate users. This decision reflects the challenges of managing trial offers.

item.title: Actions workflows are limited to 50 reruns
item.description: GitHub has imposed a limit of 50 reruns on Actions workflows, introducing stricter controls to enhance platform reliability. This change aims to prevent excessive resource use and maintain performance.

item.title: Copilot CLI activity now included in usage metrics totals and feature breakdowns
item.description: Copilot CLI activity is now included in the overall usage metrics, providing a more comprehensive view of user engagement with the tool. This update helps developers understand feature usage better.

item.title: This handy electric screwdriver is now 50% off - here's where to snag the deal
item.description: A promotion is offering a 50% discount on the Fanttik S1 Pro electric screwdriver, making it a great deal for DIY enthusiasts. This tool is ideal for small repairs and projects.

item.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
item.description: During National Robotics Week, NVIDIA is showcasing advancements in physical AI and robotics, emphasizing their impact across various industries. These innovations are accelerating the integration of AI into real-world applications.

item.title: This Ryobi yard essentials bundle packs a free power tool - how to get yours
item.description: Home Depot is offering a Ryobi yard essentials bundle that includes a free power tool, appealing to homeowners looking to enhance their gardening tools. This promotion encourages DIY landscaping.

item.title: 'Finally, a boomless headset for the office': How Jabra made headphones I'd take to a boardroom
item.description: Jabra has launched the Evolve3 75, a hybrid headset designed for business use, focusing on comfort and functionality for office environments. This product aims to meet the needs of modern hybrid workspaces.

item.title: I used this EcoFlow battery to run my 3,000-sq-ft home in a blackout - here's how it kept my AC on
item.description: The EcoFlow Delta Pro Ultra X battery can power a 3,000-sq-ft home during blackouts, keeping essential systems like AC running smoothly. This showcases advancements in home backup power solutions.

item.title: Anthropic’s Mythos Will Force a Cybersecurity Reckoning—Just Not the One You Think
item.description: Anthropic's new AI model, Mythos, is raising concerns as it could be exploited by hackers, prompting a call for better security practices among developers. Its launch signals a critical moment for cybersecurity.

item.title: Microsoft's Windows Insider Program is no longer a confusing mess
item.description: Microsoft has revamped its Windows Insider Program, making it more user-friendly and less confusing for testers. This change aims to improve the experience for those previewing Windows 11 updates.

item.title: Forget Shokz: I tried the Suunto Spark earbuds for a month, and they've sold me on air conduction
item.description: The Suunto Spark earbuds have impressed users with their air conduction technology, outperforming traditional bone conduction headphones in sound quality during workouts. This marks a shift in audio technology preferences.

item.title: iOS 26.4 brings essential upgrades to your iPhone - including a vital security fix
item.description: iOS 26.4 introduces crucial upgrades, including a vital security fix for iPhones, although users will have to wait for an improved Siri. This update emphasizes Apple's focus on security.

item.title: Microsoft's Copilot strategy is just more user abuse from Redmond, says Mozilla
item.description: Mozilla criticizes Microsoft's Copilot strategy, claiming it prioritizes corporate interests over user choice by scaling back features without adequate consideration for users. This reflects ongoing tensions in tech ethics.

item.title: Stalking victim sues OpenAI, claims ChatGPT fueled her abuser’s delusions and ignored her warnings
item.description: A lawsuit against OpenAI alleges that ChatGPT contributed to a stalking situation by ignoring warnings about a dangerous user. This case raises questions about AI's responsibility in user interactions.

item.title: TechCrunch is heading to Tokyo — and bringing the Startup Battlefield with it
item.description: TechCrunch is set to host the Startup Battlefield in Tokyo, focusing on AI, robotics, and other transformative technologies. This event will showcase innovations and discussions on their societal impacts.

item.title: Suspect Arrested for Allegedly Throwing Molotov Cocktail at Sam Altman’s Home
item.description: A suspect was arrested for allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's home, raising security concerns for tech leaders. The incident underscores the risks faced by prominent figures in the industry.

item.title: 20-year-old man arrested for allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at Sam Altman’s house
item.description: A 20-year-old man has been arrested for allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at Sam Altman's house, with the incident captured on surveillance. This event highlights security issues surrounding tech leaders.

item.title: This Startup Wants You to Pay Up to Talk With AI Versions of Human Experts
item.description: Onix is introducing a platform where users can pay to interact with AI versions of health and wellness influencers, providing advice and promoting products. This innovation could change how consumers access expert guidance.

item.title: GitHub Copilot CLI for Beginners: Getting started with GitHub Copilot CLI
item.description: A tutorial has been released for beginners on how to use GitHub Copilot CLI, guiding users through its features and functionalities. This resource aims to enhance user experience and productivity.

item.title: Electronics industry says FCC's foreign-made router policy is a bit of a mesh
item.description: The Global Electronics Association warns that the FCC's ban on foreign-made routers could limit consumer choices and delay access to advanced technology. This could hinder the adoption of next-gen Wi-Fi products.

item.title: What’s in a name? Moderna’s “vaccine” vs. “therapy” dilemma
item.description: Moderna faces a dilemma over how to label its next-generation mRNA products, as the terminology could influence public perception and acceptance. This reflects broader challenges in vaccine communication.

item.title: Last 24 hours: Save up to $500 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass
item.description: TechCrunch is offering a last chance to save up to $500 on passes for its 2026 Disrupt event, with discounts ending soon. This is a call to action for potential attendees.

item.title: The Iranian Lego AI video creators credit their virality to ‘heart’
item.description: Iranian content creators have gained attention for their viral AI-generated Lego videos, which creatively reinterpret political events. This showcases the intersection of technology and storytelling.

item.title: CPUID site hijacked to serve malware instead of HWMonitor downloads
item.description: The CPUID website was compromised, leading to a six-hour breach where malware was served instead of legitimate downloads. This incident raises concerns about cybersecurity and trust in software sources.

item.title: Copilot cloud agent’s validation tools are now 20% faster
item.description: GitHub's Copilot cloud agent has improved its validation tools, making them 20% faster in checking code quality and security. This enhancement aims to streamline the coding process for developers.

item.title: Project Glasswing and open source software: The good, the bad, and the ugly
item.description: Anthropic's Project Glasswing aims to address vulnerabilities in open source software by utilizing AI to identify and fix issues. This initiative highlights the importance of security in software development.

item.title: Britain seeks views before it drops the hammer on signal jammers
item.description: The UK government is gathering public opinions on the regulation of radiofrequency jammers, which are often linked to criminal activities. This consultation could shape future legislation.

item.title: Fear and loathing at OpenAI
item.description: Sam Altman's leadership at OpenAI has been tumultuous, marked by his brief firing and subsequent reinstatement, leading to significant organizational changes. This situation raises questions about stability and direction at the company.

item.title: OpenAI Backs Bill That Would Limit Liability for AI-Enabled Mass Deaths or Financial Disasters
item.description: OpenAI has supported an Illinois bill that would limit liability for AI companies in cases of severe harm caused by their products. This legislation could set a precedent for how AI developers are held accountable.

item.title: Research with ChatGPT
item.description: A guide on using ChatGPT for research has been published, detailing how to leverage its capabilities for finding and analyzing information effectively. This resource aims to enhance users' research skills.

item.title: Working with files in ChatGPT
item.description: An article explains how to upload and work with files in ChatGPT, enabling users to analyze data and generate content from various document types. This feature enhances the utility of ChatGPT for users.

item.title: ACM Human-Computer Interaction Conference (CHI) 2026
item.description: Apple is set to present new research at the ACM CHI Conference 2026, focusing on human-computer interaction. This event gathers experts from academia and industry to discuss advancements in the field.

item.title: Show HN: AnimTOON – AI that animates SVGs with 3-4x fewer tokens than OmniLottie
item.description: AnimTOON is an AI model that animates SVGs into Lottie files using significantly fewer tokens than existing models. This innovation could streamline the animation process for developers.

item.title: claw-code 0.2.2
item.description: The release of claw-code 0.2.2 offers a local alternative to Claude Code, eliminating API costs for users. This development provides a cost-effective solution for developers.

item.title: Vance’s ‘Fraud Czar’ Title May Come Back to Haunt Him
item.description: Vance's new role as 'Fraud Czar' may carry implications due to past controversies, potentially affecting his effectiveness in addressing fraud. This title could influence public perception and policy.

item.title: Anthropic and xAI Model Parameter Counts
item.description: Elon Musk revealed that Anthropic's Claude Sonnet and Claude Opus models have 1 trillion and 5 trillion parameters, respectively, while XAI's Grok models also feature significant parameter counts. This highlights the competitive landscape in AI model development.

item.title: Morning Headlines 4/10/26
item.description: A brief news roundup includes various headlines, including a request from Trump's personnel agency for federal workers' medical records. This highlights ongoing political and administrative developments.
